documentation_impact.rationale: Phase 4 dogfood feature-delivery review stage. The reviewer ran modern-code-review against commit 7d7b62a and emitted work/timestamp-naming-conventions/review.md with verdict pass-with-conditions: five verification gates green, all five migration safety properties pass, every spec acceptance clause maps to an executable surface, and the strict-references dry run reports zero stale rows. Two warn-severity findings landed: L1 a citation line range mismatch in the migration-precedence Spec Contract (now realigned per AGENTS.md §3 deterministic maintenance), and L2 a contentHash mixing pattern in inbox-lifecycle.md (deferred to librarian indexing for the global contentHash refresh pass). Index advanced to current_stage review with handoff to tech-writer / report.

deferred_items.rationale: inbox-lifecycle.md frontmatter mixes the new TBD-on-commit citation with computed sha256 hashes from prior passes. The librarian SHALL refresh all contentHash placeholders in a single dedicated pass; ad-hoc partial refreshes would recreate the same drift downstream.

Spec Contract execution depends on the contract-runner-rego and contract-runner-llm-judge runners being fully wired into a panel; the implement slice ships the structural surfaces; runner integration is M2 per Q18 (mcp-server) and the Phase 5 contract corpus pass.

policy_alignment.agents_md_alignment: Slice delegates the review stage to the named reviewer persona per §4 and applies the §3 deterministic-maintenance carveout for the L1 citation realignment. No spec, plan, ADR, or implement-stage source was modified outside the maintenance carveout.
policy_alignment.constitution_alignment: Slice keeps every emitted artifact dual-anchored, surfaces every Layer-1 finding loudly, and defers the cross-feature contentHash refresh to the librarian's dedicated pass to avoid drift.
policy_alignment.prd_alignment: Slice satisfies the Phase 4 review-stage requirement (modern-code-review + Spec Contract pass) and emits the canonical review.md artifact for the report stage.
